title: Google Android March 2026 Security Bulletin: Actively Exploited Qualcomm Display Driver Zero-Day (CVE-2026-21385) Plus Critical No-Interaction RCE Among 129 Vulnerabilities Patched — Largest Android Update Since April 2018
affected_products: Android OS (all versions with pre-March 2026 patch level)

sources.name: The Hacker News: Google Confirms CVE-2026-21385 in Qualcomm Android Component Exploited
CyberScoop: Google addresses actively exploited Qualcomm zero-day in fresh batch of 129 Android vulnerabilities
DEV Community: Android March 2026 Patch — 129 Flaws Fixed, Qualcomm Zero-Day Exploited
CyberPress: Android Security Update Fixes 129 Vulnerabilities Including Actively Exploited Zero-Day
Sammy Fans: Samsung's March 2026 update carries 67 urgent patches
Nokia Power User: Google's March 2026 Android Security Update Fixes 129 Vulnerabilities Including Active Zero-Day
Security Affairs: Google's latest Android security update fixes two actively exploited flaws
MITRE ATT&CK Mobile: Exploitation for Privilege Escalation (T1404)

timeline.event: CVE-2026-21385 discovered by Google Android Security team in Qualcomm display driver and reported to Qualcomm
Qualcomm notified affected customers (OEMs) about CVE-2026-21385 with patch information
Google released Android Security Bulletin for March 2026 (patch level 2026-03-01), patching 63 CVEs including CVE-2026-0006 critical RCE
Samsung released March 2026 security update with 67 patches including CVE-2026-21385 fix
Google released 2026-03-05 patch level with remaining 66 CVEs, including Qualcomm-specific fixes. CISA added CVE-2026-21385 to KEV catalog with March 10 remediation deadline

simple_summary.what_happened: Google released its largest Android security update since April 2018, patching 129 security vulnerabilities including one that attackers are already actively exploiting in the wild. The exploited vulnerability (CVE-2026-21385) is in code made by Qualcomm — the chip manufacturer that powers hundreds of millions of Android phones — and specifically affects the graphics/display hardware driver. A second critical vulnerability (CVE-2026-0006) allows hackers to install malware on a phone without the user doing anything at all, simply by being online.
simple_summary.who_is_affected: Anyone using an Android smartphone or tablet with a Qualcomm processor is potentially at risk — this covers 234 different Qualcomm chipsets found in devices from budget to flagship tier across Samsung, Google Pixel, OnePlus, Motorola, Xiaomi, and nearly every major Android brand. The critical no-interaction RCE (CVE-2026-0006) affects a broad range of Android versions.
simple_summary.what_attackers_do: Step 1: Attacker identifies a target device running Android with an unpatched Qualcomm display driver (pre-March 2026 patch level). Step 2: Attacker gains initial code execution on the device — through a malicious app, browser exploit, or the critical CVE-2026-0006 RCE which requires zero user interaction. Step 3: Attacker triggers the memory corruption bug (CVE-2026-21385) in the Qualcomm graphics driver by sending a specially crafted request to the display subsystem interface, causing an integer overflow and buffer over-read. Step 4: Memory corruption allows the attacker to overwrite critical memory regions, breaking out of the application sandbox and achieving kernel-level privileges on the device. Step 5: With full device control, the attacker can silently install persistent spyware, intercept calls, messages, and banking credentials, track the victim's location, and use the compromised device as a pivot point to attack corporate networks the phone is connected to.
what_you_should_do: Immediately apply the Android March 2026 security update on all managed Android devices — look for patch level 2026-03-05 or later in Settings > About Phone > Android Security Update
Samsung users should specifically install the March 2026 One UI update which includes 67 patches; prioritize this for enterprise-enrolled Samsung devices
In MDM/EMM consoles (Intune, VMware Workspace ONE, Jamf), enforce minimum security patch level of 2026-03-05 and mark non-compliant devices as restricted from corporate email and VPN
Audit your Android device inventory to identify all devices running Qualcomm chipsets and track patching progress
Monitor for suspicious behavior on Android endpoints: unusual app permissions, unexpected data transfers, or apps requesting graphics driver access
simple_summary.why_this_is_serious: The Qualcomm zero-day is already being weaponized in targeted attacks before most users even know it exists. With 234 different Qualcomm chip models affected, the exposure spans hundreds of millions of devices globally. For enterprises, unpatched Android devices are gateways to corporate networks — email, VPN clients, and authentication apps on compromised phones give attackers direct access to business systems. CISA has given federal agencies only 7 days (until March 10) to patch, signaling the urgency. The combination of an actively exploited privilege escalation bug plus a critical zero-click RCE in the same bulletin represents one of the most serious Android threat packages in recent memory.

tactics.technique_name: Deliver Malicious App via Other Means
tactics.description: Attackers may deliver malicious apps outside of official app stores, or exploit CVE-2026-0006 to gain initial access with zero user interaction via a drive-by attack
CVE-2026-21385 is exploited to perform privilege escalation from application sandbox to kernel level by triggering memory corruption in the Qualcomm display driver
CVE-2026-0006 enables remote code execution in the Android System component without requiring any user interaction, allowing malicious code to execute remotely
After gaining kernel-level access, attackers can install persistent implants that survive reboots by modifying system boot components
With full device compromise, attackers capture keystrokes, screen content, microphone audio, and camera data for espionage or credential harvesting

detection_guidance.summary: Detection of CVE-2026-21385 exploitation is challenging because Google and Qualcomm have not released public Indicators of Compromise. Focus on verifying patch status across all Android endpoints, monitoring MDM compliance, and watching for anomalous behavior indicative of compromised devices.

log_sources.description: Primary detection source — check compliance dashboards for devices reporting Android security patch level below 2026-03-05

log_sources.source: Android Device System Logs (via ADB or MDM)
log_sources.description: Monitor for kernel panics, GPU/display driver crashes, and SIGSEGV signals from graphics processes which may indicate exploitation attempts
MTD solutions (CrowdStrike Falcon for Mobile, Lookout, Zimperium) detect anomalous privilege escalation behavior and exploit attempts on Android devices
Monitor for unusual data exfiltration or C2 communication patterns originating from Android device IP addresses on corporate networks

what_to_look_for.description: Any device reporting a security patch date earlier than March 5, 2026 is vulnerable to CVE-2026-21385

what_to_look_for.indicator: Kernel panic or crash in display/graphics subsystem
what_to_look_for.description: Crash logs with stack traces involving Qualcomm display driver functions (mdss, msm_drm, adreno) may indicate exploitation attempts
what_to_look_for.log_field: dmesg: 'Fatal signal 11 (SIGSEGV)' OR 'BUG: kernel NULL pointer dereference' in 'adreno' OR 'msm_drm' context

what_to_look_for.indicator: Unexpected root or system-level process spawned from graphics daemon
what_to_look_for.description: Post-exploitation activity where an attacker leverages the privilege escalation to spawn elevated-privilege processes
what_to_look_for.log_field: Process audit: parent_process='surfaceflinger' OR 'android.hardware.graphics' spawning uid=0 child process

what_to_look_for.indicator: Sudden spike in cellular/WiFi data from device with outdated patch
what_to_look_for.description: C2 communication or data exfiltration following successful compromise of an unpatched device
what_to_look_for.log_field: Firewall: source_ip=<android_device> bytes_out > baseline_threshold AND device_patch_level < '2026-03-05'

immediate_actions.action: Apply March 2026 Android security patch immediately
immediate_actions.description: Navigate to Settings > System > Software Update on all Android devices and install the March 2026 security update. Ensure patch level reaches 2026-03-05 or later to include all Qualcomm-specific fixes. CISA deadline for federal agencies is March 10, 2026.
immediate_actions.command: adb shell getprop ro.build.version.security_patch  # Verify patch level

immediate_actions.action: Enforce minimum patch level via MDM/EMM
immediate_actions.description: In Microsoft Intune, VMware Workspace ONE, or Jamf, create a compliance policy requiring Android security patch level >= 2026-03-05. Mark non-compliant devices as restricted, blocking access to corporate email, VPN, and applications until patched.

Run a device inventory report in your MDM console filtering for Android devices. Qualcomm-based devices include most flagship and mid-range Android phones from Samsung (Exynos excluded), Google Pixel, OnePlus, Motorola, Xiaomi, and others. Prioritize patching for devices with access to sensitive corporate systems.

immediate_actions.action: Deploy Mobile Threat Defense for enhanced monitoring
immediate_actions.description: If not already deployed, activate a Mobile Threat Defense (MTD) solution integrated with your MDM. MTD platforms detect exploit behavior and privilege escalation attempts that standard MDM cannot see. For unpatched devices that cannot be immediately updated, MTD provides a compensating control.

immediate_actions.action: Review and revoke sensitive access for non-compliant devices
immediate_actions.description: Immediately revoke VPN certificates, corporate email profiles, and MFA tokens for Android devices that cannot be patched within 48 hours. Re-issue access only after patch verification. Pay special attention to devices used by executives, IT admins, or employees with access to financial or HR systems.
